name: Visibility Module - Current State Documentation
description: Document the current state of the visibility module including all sub-modules, data flow, architecture, design, key classes, dependencies, lambda functions, and build interactions. This is before the AWS 2.x upgrade.

entries.summary: Completed analysis of all 11 visibility sub-modules. Identified 5 Dropwizard services (inbound, matcher, outbound, pending, wm-inbound-processor, itv-gps-processor) and 4 Lambda functions (s3-archiver, error-email, pending-start, outbound-poller). All use AWS SDK v1 for DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, S3. Commons is shared library. Architecture is event-driven pipeline: inbound -> DynamoDB Stream -> matcher -> outbound SQS -> outbound processor -> S3/GIS.
entries.detail: Sub-modules: visibility-commons (shared library), visibility-inbound (REST API + SQS processor), visibility-matcher (DynamoDB stream consumer), visibility-outbound (GIS file generation), visibility-pending (retry unmatched events), visibility-wm-inbound-processor (Watermill/Shippeo events), visibility-itv-gps-processor (GPS events from ITV), visibility-s3-archiver (Lambda: archive to S3), visibility-error-email (Lambda: send error emails), visibility-pending-start (Lambda: trigger pending processing), visibility-outbound-poller (Lambda: poll MySQL outbound thresholds)

entries.summary: Completed DESIGN-curr-state.md document for the visibility module. Document covers all 11 sub-modules, architecture, data flow, AWS SDK v1 usage, and dependencies.
entries.detail:
Created comprehensive design document at visibility/docs/DESIGN-curr-state.md covering:
- Executive summary
- 11 sub-module deep dives (6 Dropwizard services, 4 Lambdas, 1 shared lib)
- High-level architecture diagram (ASCII)
- Full data flow pipeline (4 stages: inbound → matching → outbound → pending retry)
- Database & storage layer (DynamoDB tables, MySQL, Oracle, Elasticsearch, S3)
- AWS services usage matrix
- Network services integration (12 REST API clients)
- Build & deployment details (Docker + Lambda ZIP)
- Sub-module dependency matrix
- Configuration reference (SQS queues, thread pools)
- Complete AWS SDK v1 usage inventory for future upgrade planning
- Appendices: class responsibility summary, StatusEventProcessor lifecycle pattern, external dependencies

entries.summary: Task COMPLETE. Created visibility/docs/DESIGN-curr-state.md documenting the full current state of the visibility module before AWS 2.x upgrade.
entries.detail:
Deliverable: visibility/docs/DESIGN-curr-state.md

Document covers:
- Executive summary of 11 sub-modules (6 Dropwizard services, 4 Lambdas, 1 shared lib)
- ASCII architecture diagrams showing full event-driven pipeline
- 4-stage data flow: Inbound → Matching → Outbound → Pending Retry
- Deep dive into each sub-module: purpose, key classes, processing flow, dependencies
- Database layer: 3 DynamoDB tables + GSIs, MySQL OutboundThreshold, Oracle legacy, Elasticsearch
- AWS services usage matrix (DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, S3, SES, SSM, Lambda, CloudWatch)
- Network services integration (12 REST API clients to Network module)
- Build & deployment (Docker containers for services, ZIP for Lambdas, CloudFormation)
- Sub-module dependency matrix with compile-scope Maven deps
- Complete AWS SDK v1 usage inventory for upgrade planning
- Appendices: class responsibility tables, StatusEventProcessor lifecycle pattern

Key findings:
- All modules use AWS SDK v1 except SES in visibility-inbound (already v2)
- Legacy commons version 1.R.01.021 (not latest 1.0.22-SNAPSHOT)
- DynamoDB access via dynamo-client library (DynamoDBMapper wrapper)
- S3 Archiver Lambda uses DynamoDB Document API directly (not shared DAO)
- visibility-pending and visibility-wm-inbound-processor have cross-module source imports from visibility-matcher and visibility-inbound
- Model info: Claude Opus 4.6
